Yggdrasil Worktree
HomeGitHub
Docs
  • Workflows
  • Commands
  • Configuration
  • Safety

Documentation

Use worktrees without losing the plot.

Yggtree is small enough to learn quickly, but it changes a habit that is easy to get wrong: switching contexts while work is already in motion. Start with the command below, then pick the workflow that matches what you are trying to protect.

Terminal
$npx yggtree
Open the guided menu from any Git repository.

Common workflows

Choose by intent first. The command is the easy part once the workflow is clear.

Start a real task

Use this when the work deserves a branch, review, and normal Git history.

Terminal
$yggtree create feat/billing-export --base main --source remote

Creates a branch-backed worktree and bootstraps the environment.

Jump to an existing branch

Use this when you are interrupted and do not want to stash current work.

Terminal
$yggtree worktree-checkout --ref hotfix/payment-timeout

Leaves your current checkout alone and opens the selected ref in another realm.

Try a disposable idea

Use this when you want a local experiment that may never become a branch.

Terminal
$yggtree create-sandbox --carry

Carries changed files into a sandbox, then lets you apply or discard the result.

Command reference

This is the compact map. Use `yggtree` with no arguments when you want the guided version.

Create realms

yggtree create [branch]

Create a branch-backed task worktree.

yggtree worktree-checkout [name] [ref]

Create a worktree from an existing branch or ref.

yggtree create-sandbox

Create a local-only sandbox for experiments.

yggtree create-multi

Create multiple official task worktrees when explicitly needed.

Move around

yggtree list

Show repo-linked worktrees and their current state.

yggtree open [worktree]

Open a worktree in an IDE or agent CLI.

yggtree enter [worktree]

Enter a worktree sub-shell.

yggtree path [worktree]

Print the cd command for a worktree.

Maintain realms

yggtree exec [worktree] -- <command>

Run a command inside a chosen worktree.

yggtree bootstrap

Run the configured setup commands again.

yggtree close

Exit an Yggdrasil sub-shell and optionally delete the worktree.

yggtree delete

Delete managed worktrees interactively.

yggtree prune

Clean stale Git worktree metadata.

Sandbox transfer

yggtree apply

Apply sandbox file changes back to the origin checkout.

yggtree unapply

Restore origin files from the sandbox backup.

Configuration

If a repository needs something other than the fallback `npm install`, define setup commands once and let every new realm inherit the same ritual.

.yggtree/worktree-setup.json

{
  "setup-worktree": [
    "pnpm install",
    "git submodule sync --recursive",
    "git submodule update --init --recursive"
  ]
}

Safety notes

Use `create` for official task branches. Use `create-sandbox` for disposable alternatives.

Run `unapply` before deleting a sandbox if you may need to undo changes applied back to the origin.

Prefer `worktree-checkout` over stash when the current checkout contains work you do not want to disturb.
